Okay, so welcome back. We are now in part two of our CTC enrollment process. This is all about actualizing all the potential that is now liberated from unpacking that situation on Gestalt. So this lesson is going to be the source code. What is actually going on in this phase of the call and how can we actually coach those milestones that we are going to be working towards. So the result of having unpacked that situation on Gestalt means that your pre-client has been successfully coached into a sense of renewed possibility and confidence along with the resourcefulness to move towards it and the resonance with you as their coach, as their leader, as their educator, as their facilitator to lead it. So these are the milestones that have been achieved from part one in unpacking that Gestalt and now it's time to actualize that potential. Because until a decision is made, all of this simply exists as a potentiality and potential unless actualized actually becomes a source of stress, of disempowerment, of anxiety, to have all this potential that actually isn't being channeled into a desirable result, that's actually more of a stressful predicament than it is an exciting one. The truth is most people will only actualize a small portion of their ever-expending potential and while by nature will only ever materialize a small fraction of it, your role, your responsibility as their conversion coach is to help them in transmuting all that potential into action. So life-changing decisions is what serves as that bridge or as that gateway between the potential and the actualization of it. Your coaching decisiveness becomes your most critical task in this second phase of your sales call. So we do that by coaching the precursors to decisiveness. What makes a decision natural, easy, and effortless is a sense of achievability, of accountability, and finally actualization, right? The action of actually, the action of actually making good on your sense of achievability, on your accountability to making a decision, making good on it, and turning that into a real world action. So let's break these down a little bit further, achievability. So results and outcomes feel achievable or even inevitable as your pre-client or pre-customer future paces themselves doing it with your method or your process. So you want to coach and instill in this phase of the call a sense of I can't lose, with your help, with your program, I can't lose. It's almost an inevitable game that they get to play. And we're most likely to play the games we are confident we can win or the ones that we feel we can't lose. So how do we coach achievability throughout this conversation? We do it through affirmation, right? Reminding them often of why you legitimately see them succeeding in your program, with the why being forms of what I call exclusive empowerment, right? What is it about the milestones they've achieved, the achievements in their lives, the abilities and capacities that they've demonstrated to you that uniquely qualify them, enable them and empower them to channel that into results on the other side of the call. We do this with what I call integrative future pacing. So when describing elements of your program or offer, we're not reading it as information. We're not sending them kind of a verbal pamphlet that we're walking them through, but really giving them a play by play of what their next eight to 12 weeks will look like. We're involving them in the story and integrating all the other elements of the gestalt that they put on the table. So that our program isn't this vague thing that isn't connected to their life, but we're actually connecting everything they shared about the gestalt into the way we explain our program and future pace, their involvement within it. So we're going to talk a lot more about that a little later on. And we're downplaying difficulty. So obviously not doing this in any kind of unethical, inauthentic way, but acknowledging that it requires commitment, right? But also saying that success is a natural, easy byproduct of your work together, right? As it should be, if your program is effective, then going through it and being led through it should naturally lead to success, right? So we could do this by bringing up similar case studies of others who had similar starting points to them. And the reason we have to affirm this frequently, right? And downplay the difficulty is to counterbalance the fact that most people will assume that the program and the success they want is more difficult than it really is. So this is really important and this makes sense, right? People will assume it's more difficult than it is because they have a history of struggle in trying to achieve it. So making that success feel like a natural, easy byproduct of your work together is a very important part, sorry, in coaching achievability. Next one you are going to be coaching as a milestone is a sense of accountability. So I call this the 100% close. And this is based on the premise that the reason most sales are lost isn't due to people giving a conscious no, but in people simply saying nothing at all, right? So we are not delaying, deferring the decision altogether and not deciding, right? This is really the biggest reason why conversions are lost. So what we do is we compassionately yet powerfully hold people to a binary yes or no decision, right? It may be on this call itself or it might be later through the Tyler Dirt and follow-up strategy that we're going to talk about later, but we don't let them dishonor themselves or overstep our boundaries, right? So in all of the energy we're investing in them by staying in this land of indecisiveness. So to me this is an ethical boundary that we get to maintain for both ourselves and our clients. And to whatever degree we can ethically do so, encouraging them to make a decision and giving them all the tools they need to do so. Cool. And the result, more empowered decisions, whether that's a yes or no will equal more empowered yeses just by the sheer mathematics of getting more decisions. So how do we coach accountability to a decision while we coach them to honoring their yes or no, right? Very simple, but make each one feel equally safe and acceptable without this pressure, right? That you're going to manipulate them, that you're going to pressure them, or even their fear of disappointing you, right? These are the things that make someone literally afraid to make a decision. So coaching them through the acceptability and the safety in owning any answer that is true and real to them is one way you coach accountability to a decision. Next, providing appropriate time and space for a decision, right? Not being afraid of letting them off the call, right? Not having this insecurity where if they escape from the call, they're never coming back and you're going to lose the sale, right? If that's your relationship to them, once again, ask what are they trying to escape from, right? So be available and be free and be even encouraging of giving them the space they need in their process to make a great decision. And you could do this by creating impeccable follow-up systems that give them a chance to do what they need to do without you fearing that you're not going to be able to close that loop. Next, simple, be explicit in asking for a decision, right? So at the same time, be clear that your expectation, you know, and your hope and your request to them is that a decision is ultimately made for your clarity and boundaries and for their own. And that you, of course, are available to support it however you can, right? So this might come up in an explicit ask, right? For both our clarity, is it reasonable there possible to ask for your decision by Thursday, right? So be clear in asking people if they could make a decision, right? And set a deadline for that decision and make yourself available to empower it in any way necessary. Next, actualization. Oop. So actualization is similar to achievability, right? By creating a resonant future-paced experience that they adopt as their new situation, they are actually stepping into an actualized version of that program, right? Another way we actualize decisions is through what I call co-creative closes. And we're going to go much deeper in this. And this is essentially giving them a chance to create, to actualize certain elements of the offer with you, right? Folks will feel more invested, more actualized in things they've had ahead in creating. You coach actualization when you walk them through post-purchase elements, right? If you give them a guided tour of the membership area. If you show them where to sign up on Circle for their membership sites. If you have them download the list of the Zoom coaching calls, right? All of these are elements of the post-purchase that can be actualized, right? Showing them what actions they'll be taking, walking them through the actualized moments of a post-purchase state is a great way to coach it. And if they are taking a few days to think about it, which will happen often, you can grant them access to the first module, the onboarding video. The onboarding questionnaire so that they can experience themselves in that post-purchase state or actualized decision. This is a really valuable tool that you can use to your advantage, right? To give people a sense of momentum in your program and the actualized state of being in it while they're still considering their most appropriate steps. So that is what we have. This is the source code for this module. We're going to go deeper into all of these. And we're going to go deeper in talking about how to share the integrated offer, right? How to actually present and walk them through the different elements while integrating their experience into it. We're going to talk about how to present investment options with confidence, making that a non-awkward, non-cringy moment that a lot of people dread. And then we're going to talk about challenges that show up in this phase of the call. How to address concerns, how to address objections, and how to lead with those co-creative closes we alluded to before. We're going to talk about ethical forms of urgency and go deeper into the 100% close and then give you some next steps to really refine and enhance your abilities in integrating these elements. So take a breather and I will meet you in the next lesson. Bye for now.
